tool_args.label: Woodhaven / Cross Bay Boulevards Q52/53 Select Bus Service Frequently Asked Questions

payload.title: Woodhaven / Cross Bay Boulevards Q52/53 Select Bus Service Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)
payload.description: Frequently Asked Questions document about the Q52/Q53 Select Bus Service project on Woodhaven and Cross Bay Boulevards

warnings: source.publisher is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Publisher has 39 accepted/canonical observations and should link to entity context when useful. Suggested relation: published_by.

tool_args.label: New York City Department of Transportation (NYCDOT)

payload.entity_name: New York City Department of Transportation (NYCDOT)
payload.description: Joint lead for the Woodhaven/Cross Bay Boulevard Select Bus Service project

tool_args.label: Metropolitan Transportation Authority (MTA)

payload.description: Joint lead for the Woodhaven/Cross Bay Boulevard Select Bus Service project

tool_args.label: Woodhaven / Cross Bay Boulevards Q52/Q53 Select Bus Service Project

payload.project_name: Woodhaven / Cross Bay Boulevards Q52/Q53 Select Bus Service
payload.description: Joint NYCDOT and MTA project to implement Select Bus Service on Woodhaven and Cross Bay Boulevards with bus lanes, off-board fare payment, transit signal priority, and pedestrian safety improvements
payload.lead_agency: New York City Department of Transportation (NYCDOT)

warnings: project.lead_agency is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Lead agency is relation context. Suggested relation: implemented_by.
project.partner_agency is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Partner agency is relation context. Suggested relation: has_partner.
project.routes_served is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Route lists should become project-route relations when supported. Suggested relation: serves_route.

tool_args.label: Woodhaven Boulevard

payload.description: Vision Zero priority corridor in Queens, part of the Q52/Q53 SBS project corridor

tool_args.label: Cross Bay Boulevard

payload.description: Vision Zero priority corridor in Queens, part of the Q52/Q53 SBS project corridor

tool_args.label: Q52 Select Bus Service

payload.description: Select Bus Service route on Woodhaven/Cross Bay Boulevards, Arverne to Queens Center Mall

tool_args.label: Q53 Select Bus Service

payload.description: Select Bus Service route on Woodhaven/Cross Bay Boulevards, Rockaway Park to Woodside

tool_args.label: Main Road Bus Lanes on Woodhaven Boulevard

payload.description: Bus lanes next to the median on the main roadway, selected as Concept 2 for Woodhaven Boulevard

tool_args.label: Offset Bus Lanes on Cross Bay Boulevard

payload.description: Bus lanes one lane away from the curb, selected as Concept 1 for Cross Bay Boulevard where right-of-way is too narrow for main road lanes

tool_args.label: Off-Board Fare Payment

payload.description: Passengers pay with MetroCard or coins at sidewalk machines before boarding, allowing boarding through any door

tool_args.label: Transit Signal Priority (TSP)

payload.description: GPS tracking of buses to extend green signals or shorten reds, plus corridor-wide signal coordination

tool_args.label: 24/7 Bus Lanes on Woodhaven and Cross Bay Boulevards

payload.description: Proposed bus lanes in effect 24 hours per day, 7 days per week
payload.location_text: Woodhaven Boulevard and Cross Bay Boulevard

normalized_location.raw_text: Woodhaven Boulevard and Cross Bay Boulevard

tool_args.label: Median Bus Stops on Woodhaven Boulevard

payload.description: Median bus islands with shelters, seating, real-time information, and wheelchair accessible waiting areas

tool_args.label: Bus Bulbs on Cross Bay Boulevard

payload.description: Sidewalk extensions at bus stops on Cross Bay Boulevard for SBS buses, allowing passing at all SBS stops

tool_args.label: Phase 1 Short-Term Improvements Planned for 2017

payload.description: First phase of short-term improvements using in-house DOT and MTA resources such as paint, bollards, and concrete islands to construct key transit and safety improvements, plus improved fare payment, new bus service plans, and transit signal priority

tool_args.label: Speed Limit Changed to 30 mph (June 2015)

payload.description: Speed limit changed to 30 mph on Woodhaven Boulevard (from 35 mph) and on Cross Bay Boulevard (from 40 mph)

tool_args.label: SBS Travel Time Improvement 15-23%

payload.raw_value_text: 15 to 23 percent improvement in travel time
payload.description: Experience on implemented SBS routes has shown a 15 to 23 percent improvement in travel time

tool_args.label: Estimated Q52/Q53 SBS 25-35% Faster on 6-mile Corridor

payload.raw_value_text: 25-35% faster on the 6-mile portion of the corridor along Woodhaven and Cross Bay Boulevard
payload.scope: 6-mile portion of corridor with bus lanes and roadway improvements
payload.description: Estimated SBS buses will travel 25-35% faster on the 6-mile portion with bus lanes and roadway improvements

tool_args.label: Estimated 10% Faster on Portions Without Bus Lanes

payload.raw_value_text: approximately 10% faster due to off-board fare payment and signal priority
payload.scope: portions of corridor without bus lanes
payload.description: NYCDOT and MTA expect buses to operate approximately 10% faster on portions without bus lanes due to off-board fare payment and signal priority

tool_args.label: Over 30,000 Bus Trips Per Day on the Corridor

payload.raw_value_text: over 30,000 bus trips each day
payload.description: Woodhaven Boulevard/Cross Bay Boulevard carries over 30,000 bus trips each day

tool_args.label: 400,000 People Within 15-Minute Walk of the Corridor

payload.raw_value_text: Approximately 400,000 people live within a 15 minute walk of the Woodhaven and Cross Bay Boulevard SBS corridor
payload.scope: half-mile radius around Q52 and Q53 corridor (Woodside to the Rockaways)
payload.description: Approximately 400,000 people live within a 15 minute walk of the SBS corridor

tool_args.label: 43% of Households Do Not Own a Car Within Walking Distance

payload.raw_value_text: 43 percent do not own a car
payload.scope: households within a 15-minute walk (1/2 mile) of the corridor
payload.description: 43% of households within a 15-minute walk of the corridor do not own a car

tool_args.label: 60% of Residents Commute by Transit Within Walking Distance

payload.raw_value_text: 60 percent of residents commute to work by transit
payload.scope: residents within a 15-minute walk (1/2 mile) of the corridor
payload.description: 60 percent of residents within a 15-minute walk of the corridor commute to work by transit

tool_args.label: 48-80% Reduction in Fare Evasion on SBS Routes

payload.raw_value_text: 48-80 percent reduction in fare evasion compared to the prior condition
payload.description: On SBS routes, there has been a 48-80 percent reduction in fare evasion compared to the prior condition

tool_args.label: 22 Fatalities and Over 3,000 Injuries on the Corridor (2009-2013)

payload.raw_value_text: 22 fatalities and over 3,000 injuries from Queens Boulevard to the Addabbo Bridge from 2009-2013
payload.scope: Queens Boulevard to the Addabbo Bridge, 2009-2013
payload.description: There have been 22 fatalities and over 3,000 injuries from Queens Boulevard to the Addabbo Bridge from 2009-2013

tool_args.label: 31 KSI per Mile on the Corridor

payload.raw_value_text: 31 people killed or severely injured (KSI) per mile
payload.scope: 6.3-mile corridor from Queens Boulevard to the Addabbo Bridge
payload.description: 195 people were killed or severely injured on the 6.3-mile corridor from 2009-2013, or 31 KSI per mile
payload.location: Queens Boulevard to the Addabbo Bridge

location_normalized.raw_text: Queens Boulevard to the Addabbo Bridge

tool_args.label: Over One-Third of People Traveling Northbound on Woodhaven at Myrtle Ave are Bus Passengers (AM Rush)

payload.raw_value_text: over one-third of people traveling northbound on Woodhaven Boulevard at Myrtle Avenue are bus passengers
payload.scope: northbound at Woodhaven Boulevard & Myrtle Avenue, AM rush hour
payload.description: Over one-third of people traveling northbound on Woodhaven Boulevard at Myrtle Avenue are bus passengers

tool_args.label: Left-Turn Restriction Locations

payload.claim_text: As of December 2015, left turn restrictions are proposed for 62 Rd (SB), Penelope Avenue (SB and NB), 64 Rd (NB and SB), 67 Rd (SB), Metropolitan Avenue (SB and NB), Union Turnpike (SB), Myrtle Avenue (SB), Forest Park Drive (NB), Jamaica Avenue (NB and SB), 101 Avenue (SB), Rockaway Boulevard (SB), Pitkin Avenue (NB and SB), and 157 Avenue (NB and SB). New left turns are proposed for 85 Road (NB), 86 Road (SB), and 103 Avenue (NB and SB).
payload.description: Proposed left-turn restrictions and new left-turn additions at specific intersections along Woodhaven and Cross Bay Boulevards
